bernhard-riemann.name: Bernhard Riemann
bernhard-riemann.bio: German mathematician who formulated the Riemann Hypothesis (1859), the foundational conjecture in analytic number theory.
bernhard-riemann.key_contribution: The Riemann Hypothesis: all nontrivial zeros of ζ(s) lie on the critical line Re(s) = 1/2

riemann.name: Bernhard Riemann
riemann.bio: German mathematician who formulated the Riemann Hypothesis (1859), foundational to analytic number theory.
riemann.key_contribution: The Riemann Hypothesis: ζ zeros on critical line Re(s)=1/2

hadamard.name: Jacques Hadamard
hadamard.bio: French mathematician who proved the Prime Number Theorem using zeta function methods.
hadamard.key_contribution: Zero-free region for ζ(s); Hadamard factorization product

de_la_vallee_poussin.name: Charles-Jean de la Vallée Poussin
de_la_vallee_poussin.bio: Belgian mathematician; joint proof of Prime Number Theorem and classical zero-free regions.
de_la_vallee_poussin.key_contribution: DVP zero-free region; classical bounds on Möbius sums

nyman.name: Bertil Nyman
nyman.bio: Swedish mathematician who reformulated RH in terms of Hilbert space closure (1950).
nyman.key_contribution: Nyman-Beurling criterion: RH ⟺ unit fractions dense in L²(0,∞)

beurling.name: Arne Beurling
beurling.bio: Swedish mathematician who formalized Nyman's Hilbert space intuition into the Nyman-Beurling criterion.
beurling.key_contribution: Closure characterization; shift from complex analysis to functional analysis

vasyunin.name: Aleksandr Vasyunin
vasyunin.bio: Russian mathematician; proved explicit cotangent bounds making Nyman-Beurling testable computationally.
vasyunin.key_contribution: Vasyunin identity: explicit cotangent sum bounds O(1/log N)

baez_duarte.name: Luis Báez-Duarte
baez_duarte.bio: Venezuelan mathematician who developed BBLS formulation, making RH equivalent to computational bounds.
baez_duarte.key_contribution: BBLS chain: period-function reduction to explicit Möbius bounds

balazard.name: Michel Balazard
balazard.bio: French mathematician; co-author of BBLS formulation (Balazard, Saias, Landreau, Saias).

saias.name: Éric Saias
saias.bio: French mathematician; co-author of BBLS formulation and modern cotangent bounds.

landreau.name: Bernard Landreau
landreau.bio: French mathematician; co-author of BBLS chain formulation.
landreau.key_contribution: Integration of classical and modern approaches

robin.name: Guy Robin
robin.bio: French mathematician who proved RH equivalent to bound on divisor sum ratios.
robin.key_contribution: Robin's inequality: σ(n)/n log log n bounds

lagarias.name: Jeffrey C. Lagarias
lagarias.bio: American mathematician; catalogued RH equivalences and analytic bounds.
lagarias.key_contribution: Elementary problem equivalent to RH via arithmetic functions

bettin.name: Sandro Bettin
bettin.bio: Italian mathematician; studied bilinear cotangent correlations and cancellation phenomena.
bettin.key_contribution: Bettin-Conrey cotangent reciprocity and BCF asymptotic

conrey.name: J. Brian Conrey
conrey.bio: American mathematician; director of AIM; expert on RH heuristics and explicit bounds.
conrey.key_contribution: BCF Mellin asymptotic; zeta correlations and moment problems

farmer.name: David W. Farmer
farmer.bio: American mathematician; computational expert on zeta zeros and analytic techniques.

montgomery.name: Hugh Montgomery
montgomery.bio: American mathematician; pioneer of pair correlation conjecture and spectral approaches to RH.
montgomery.key_contribution: Montgomery-Odlyzko law; eigenvalue spacing of ζ zeros

odlyzko.name: Andrew Odlyzko
odlyzko.bio: American mathematician; numerical expert on zeta zeros; validated GUE hypothesis.
odlyzko.key_contribution: Numerical verification of zero spacing distributions

polya.name: George Pólya
polya.bio: Hungarian-American mathematician; conjectured hidden operator with eigenvalues = ζ zeros.
polya.key_contribution: Spectral approach: operator eigenvalues and RH

hilbert.name: David Hilbert
hilbert.bio: German mathematician; formulated RH as one of 23 millennium problems; inspired spectral approaches.

nyman_reference.name: Nils Nyman
nyman_reference.bio: Finnish mathematician; original author of Nyman-Beurling Hilbert space criterion.
nyman_reference.key_contribution: Functional reformulation of RH via closure in L²

berry.name: Michael Berry
berry.bio: British physicist; proposed connection between ζ zeros and quantum chaos.

snaith.name: Nina Snaith
snaith.bio: British mathematician; expert on random matrix theory and GUE zero statistics.
snaith.key_contribution: Probabilistic models of ζ zero distribution

beck.name: Matthias Beck
beck.bio: German-American mathematician; expert on finite trigonometric character sums.
beck.key_contribution: Character sum bounds; discrete Fourier analysis

auli.name: Amine Auli
auli.bio: Moroccan mathematician; modern study of Estermann sums and reciprocity.

bayad.name: Abdelmejid Bayad
bayad.bio: Moroccan-French mathematician; Estermann kernel and period function theory.

belhadj.name: Nasreddine Belhadj
belhadj.bio: Algerian mathematician; digamma and cotangent function bounds.

goubi.name: Hamid Goubi
goubi.bio: Algerian mathematician; cotangent and period function identities.

iwaniec.name: Henryk Iwaniec
iwaniec.bio: Polish-American mathematician; pioneer in sieve methods and analytic number theory with explicit bounds.
iwaniec.key_contribution: Zero-free regions and explicit bounds via sieve methods

kowalski.name: Emmanuel Kowalski
kowalski.bio: Swiss mathematician; expert on Mellin transforms, exponential sums, and analytic number theory.
kowalski.key_contribution: Mellin transform techniques in analytic number theory

chebyshev.name: Pafnuty Chebyshev
chebyshev.bio: Russian mathematician; foundational work on distribution of primes and polynomial approximation.
chebyshev.key_contribution: Chebyshev bounds on prime counting function

hardy.name: Godfrey Hardy
hardy.bio: English mathematician; proved infinitely many RH zeros on critical line, pioneered analytic number theory.
hardy.key_contribution: Hardy-Littlewood circle method and zero-detection on critical line

littlewood.name: John Littlewood
littlewood.bio: English mathematician; collaborated with Hardy on prime number theory and RH-related bounds.
littlewood.key_contribution: Hardy-Littlewood methods for analytic number theory

titchmarsh.name: Edward Titchmarsh
titchmarsh.bio: English mathematician; expert on zeta function theory and critical-line zeros.
titchmarsh.key_contribution: Zeta function theory and lower bounds on zero counts

ramanujan.name: Srinivasa Ramanujan
ramanujan.bio: Indian mathematician; discovered remarkable identities related to divisor sums and RH heuristics.
